title: Agents and Multi-Step Systems
shortSummary: Move from one-shot responses to supervised systems that plan, call tools, recover from failure, and ask for approval when needed.
whyItMatters: Agentic systems are now a real engineering surface, not just a demo pattern. Strong engineers know how to constrain autonomy, log decisions, and design loops that can survive the messiness of production.
coreConcepts: Tool execution, retries, and bounded permissions
Memory and context handling across steps
Human approval checkpoints for risky actions
Tracing agent decisions for debugging and trust
